pattern: Depth-first search from every cell, marking the current cell visited during recursion and restoring it on backtrack, tests whether the word forms a connected path.
hints: The word must trace a path of orthogonally adjacent cells where no cell is reused, so a search that grows the path one letter at a time fits.
Start a search from any cell matching the first letter, then try the four neighbors for the next letter, undoing your mark when a branch fails.
Recurse with the next index into the word; mark the cell so the same path cannot revisit it, recurse into the four directions, and unmark before returning so other paths can use it.

edge_inputs.description: would need to reuse a cell, so absent
word longer than the grid can hold
snake path through the whole board

stress.description: small grids over a tiny alphabet with words that may or may not exist
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, 6)
    n = rng.randint(1, 6)
    alpha = 'ABC'
    board = [[rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    wlen = rng.randint(1, 8)
    word = ''.join(rng.choice(alpha) for _ in range(wlen))
    return [board, word]
stress.note: tiny alphabet makes real paths common so both true and false outcomes occur
